synthesis_text: COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected individuals. Infected individuals exhale virus-containing particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing. Transmission is most likely at close range but can occur over longer distances, especially indoors. COVID-19 can also spread when infectious particles come into contact with the eyes, nose, or mouth. Additionally, touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can lead to transmission. People can remain contagious for up to 20 days and may spread the virus without showing symptoms.

COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through close contact, primarily via tiny droplets released when an infected person coughs, sneezes, talks, or breathes [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]. Infected individuals can transmit the virus even if they do not show symptoms [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]. The risk of transmission increases in areas with low airflow and crowded spaces [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00011]. Additionally, the duration of contact with an infected person affects the likelihood of transmission [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00011]. Touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can also lead to infection [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00004].

COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through close contact, primarily via small droplets and aerosols expelled when an infected person coughs, sneezes, talks, sings, or breathes. These infectious particles can be inhaled by others or come into contact with their eyes, nose, or mouth. Additionally, individuals can become infected by touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching their face. The virus can also spread in areas with low airflow, increasing the risk of transmission. Importantly, people can be contagious even if they do not exhibit symptoms [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00003][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00000][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00005][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00029][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012].
